title: China 11-Day Itinerary
summary: Shanghai start, Zhangjiajie and Fenghuang rail hops, Avatar peaks, Tianmen Mountain, Beijing classics, then Taipei.

stays.label: Homeinn Selected Hotel (Nanjing Road)
stays.address: No. 11, Lane 135, Jiangxi Middle Road, Huangpu District, Shanghai, 200002, China
stays.note: Walking distance to The Bund and Nanjing Road East. Use this base for the Shanghai city days and the Pudong robotaxi morning.
stays.transit: Nanjing East Road Station (Lines 2 and 10) is a short walk. From PVG, take Metro Line 2 directly, or the Maglev to Longyang Road then Line 2.

stays.label: Tianqingshe Riverview Homestay
stays.address: No. 70 Jinping Road, Tuojiang Town, Fenghuang County, Hunan, China
stays.note: Riverside guesthouse inside the ancient town. Perfect for the lantern-lit evening walk along the Tuojiang River.
stays.transit: From Zhangjiajie Hehua (DYG), taxi to Zhangjiajie West Railway Station, take the high-speed train to Fenghuanggucheng, then shuttle or taxi into the old town.

stays.label: Zhinanzhen Boutique Inn
stays.address: Guanliping Resettlement Community, Yongding District, Zhangjiajie, Hunan, China
stays.note: Close to the park access roads. Use this base for the National Forest Park day and Tianmen Mountain.
stays.transit: About 30 to 45 minutes by taxi from Zhangjiajie Hehua Airport (DYG). Park shuttles connect to Wulingyuan and Tianmen.

stays.label: PiaoHome Chain Hotel (Guomao Donghua)
stays.address: No. 1-6, Building 4, Chaijiawan, Chaoyang District, Beijing, 100025, China
stays.note: Chaoyang base for the Beijing classics. Late check-in expected because the Zhangjiajie flight lands after midnight.
stays.transit: Closest stations are Sihui and Sihui East (Lines 1 and Batong). Quick taxi to CBD and Wangfujing. Daxing Airport is about 60 to 75 minutes by taxi.

image_queries: Shanghai Bund Pudong skyline blue hour

days.label: Day 1
days.focus: Travel day. Long-haul to Pudong with one stop, lands the next morning.
days.transport_note: Pack light carry-on items for the layover. Eat and sleep on the second leg if you can.

days.label: Day 2
days.display_title: Shanghai arrival, hotel, Bund and Nanjing Road
days.focus: Check in beside Nanjing Road East, then ease into Shanghai with the Bund, pedestrian street lights, and coffee at the Roastery.
days.transport_note: Stay on foot once you check in. Hotel is steps from the Bund and Nanjing Road East.

start_transfer.title: PVG Airport to Hotel
start_transfer.instructions: From Pudong Airport, take Metro Line 2 toward downtown and exit at Nanjing East Road Station. The hotel is a short walk down Jiangxi Middle Road. The Maglev to Longyang Road then Line 2 is faster if you want to skip the long stretch.
start_transfer.note: Taxis from PVG run about 60 to 80 minutes in Friday traffic. Metro is the most reliable arrival option.

places.name: The Bund
places.summary: Riverside promenade lined with European-era facades on one side and the Pudong skyline across the water. The classic Shanghai postcard view.
places.transport: Walk from the hotel along Nanjing Road East to the waterfront. Or take Metro Line 2 to Nanjing East Road Station.

foods.name: Shengjianbao
foods.description: Pan-fried pork buns with a crisp golden bottom, soft top, and a small burst of broth inside.
foods.where: Yang's Fry Dumplings (Xiaoyang Shengjian) on Wujiang Road or the Huanghe Road branch.

places.name: Nanjing Road Walking Street
places.summary: Shanghai's classic shopping spine from People's Square toward the Bund. Use the East Nanjing pedestrian stretch for neon, snacks, and an easy first-night walk.
highlights: Historic department stores and bright signage
Simple walk back to the hotel
places.transport: Walk from the hotel. The main pedestrian section starts around Henan Middle Road and runs west toward People's Square.

images.title: File:2024-Apr Shanghai East Nanjing Road morning 01.jpg

places.name: Starbucks Reserve Roastery 星巴克臻选上海烘焙工坊
places.area: West Nanjing Road, Jing'an District, Shanghai
places.summary: Huge Shanghai Reserve Roastery with copper roasting casks, coffee bars, tea drinks, and bakery counters. Good low-effort stop after the first city walk.
places.transport: Metro Line 2 from Nanjing East Road to West Nanjing Road, then walk a few minutes to HKRI Taikoo Hui.

transfers.instructions: Walk from the Bund into Nanjing Road Walking Street and follow the lights west.
transfers.note: Keep this relaxed after the long-haul arrival.

transfers.instructions: Ride Line 2 from Nanjing East Road to West Nanjing Road for the Roastery.
transfers.note: Taxi or Didi is easier if jet lag is catching up.

stay.label: Homeinn Selected Hotel (Nanjing Road)
stay.address: No. 11, Lane 135, Jiangxi Middle Road, Huangpu District, Shanghai, 200002, China
stay.note: Walking distance to The Bund and Nanjing Road East. Use this base for the Shanghai city days and the Pudong robotaxi morning.
stay.transit: Nanjing East Road Station (Lines 2 and 10) is a short walk. From PVG, take Metro Line 2 directly, or the Maglev to Longyang Road then Line 2.

days.label: Day 3
days.display_title: Shanghai gardens, lanes and Jing'an Temple
days.focus: Old Shanghai at Yu Garden, French Concession lanes at Tianzifang, then Jing'an Temple and West Nanjing Road.
days.transport_note: Use Metro Lines 10, 9, and 2. Keep the day city-focused before the Pudong robotaxi morning tomorrow.

start_transfer.title: Hotel to Yu Garden
start_transfer.instructions: Walk south through Huangpu about 15 to 20 minutes, or take Line 10 to Yuyuan Garden Station and exit toward the bazaar.
start_transfer.note: Arrive close to opening to skip the worst of the bus tour crowd.

places.name: Yu Garden and Yuyuan Bazaar
places.summary: Ming-era classical garden surrounded by a busy bazaar of pavilions, snack stalls, and the famous Huxinting teahouse on the zigzag bridge.
highlights: Huxinting Teahouse on the zigzag bridge
Nanxiang Steamed Bun stall in the bazaar
places.transport: Metro Line 10 to Yuyuan Garden Station, Exit 1 or 3. Walk through the bazaar to the garden entrance.

foods.name: Xiaolongbao
foods.description: Steamed soup dumplings with a thin pleated wrap and a hot pork-and-broth filling. Bite the side, sip the soup, then eat.
foods.where: Nanxiang Steamed Bun Restaurant on the Yuyuan Bazaar bridge, or any branch of Din Tai Fung in Shanghai.

images.title: File:Yank Sing Shanghai Dumpling on Soup Spoon (24441687126).jpg

places.name: Tianzifang
places.summary: Maze of shikumen lane houses turned into small studios, cafes, and craft shops. The most photogenic slice of the old French Concession.
highlights: Easy walking grid in a small footprint
places.transport: Metro Line 9 to Dapuqiao Station, Exit 1. Tianzifang sits across the road.

foods.name: Cong You Ban Mian (Scallion Oil Noodles)
foods.description: Chewy noodles tossed with crispy scallions, soy, and dark vinegar. Cheap, fragrant, and very Shanghainese.
foods.where: Wei Xiang Zhai near People's Square, or any neighborhood noodle shop in the French Concession.

places.name: Jing'an Temple
places.summary: Active Buddhist temple wrapped by one of Shanghai's busiest commercial districts. The gold roofs against modern towers make it one of the city's best contrasts.
highlights: West Nanjing Road skyline around the temple
places.transport: Metro Line 2 or 7 to Jing'an Temple Station. The temple entrance is directly above the station area.

transfers.instructions: From Yu Garden, ride Line 10 to South Shaanxi Road and transfer to Line 9 for Dapuqiao and Tianzifang.
transfers.note: A short Didi is also fine between these older districts.

transfers.instructions: From Dapuqiao, ride Line 9 to Century Avenue and transfer to Line 2 westbound for Jing'an Temple.
transfers.note: West Nanjing Road is easy for dinner or coffee after the temple.

days.optional_title: Extra Shanghai Stops If Energy Is High

optional_places.name: Shanghai Museum
optional_places.summary: One of China's best museums for bronzes, ceramics, calligraphy, painting, jade, and furniture. It is the easiest high-value indoor add-on in central Shanghai.
highlights: Good weather backup near People's Square
optional_places.transport: Metro Line 1, 2, or 8 to People's Square, then walk through the square. Reserve ahead if timed entry is required.

optional_places.name: Xintiandi
optional_places.summary: Restored shikumen lane-house district with cafes, restaurants, and polished nightlife. Works well after Jing'an or Tianzifang if you want a cleaner dinner area.
highlights: Easy link to French Concession walks
optional_places.transport: Metro Line 10/13 to Xintiandi Station, or short taxi from Tianzifang/Jing'an.

days.label: Day 4
days.display_title: Pudong self-driving cars and Lujiazui icons
days.focus: Spend the morning in Pudong New Area trying a robotaxi, then cover the Oriental Pearl deck, Mingzhu Roundabout, and the Lujiazui skyline.
days.transport_note: Robotaxi pickup/drop-off is by designated stop inside the service zone, not anywhere like a normal Didi. Some pilots run weekday-only, so confirm in-app and swap this with a weekday Shanghai block if needed.

start_transfer.title: Hotel to Pudong New Area robotaxi zone
start_transfer.instructions: Ride Line 2 east from Nanjing East Road toward Pudong. For the central Pudong pilot areas, start around Jinqiao/Huamu or Century Park; Yunshun Road and Century Park are useful anchors depending on the app coverage that day.
start_transfer.note: Open Apollo Go (萝卜快跑), PonyPilot+ / Pony.ai (小马智行), Dazhong app, or Gaode/AMap after you are physically in the service area. Look for robotaxi / 无人车 options and choose an available designated pickup point.

places.name: Pudong New Area Self-Driving Car Trial
places.area: Jinqiao, Huamu and Century Park area, Pudong, Shanghai
places.summary: Shanghai robotaxi pilots operate in defined Pudong zones, especially Jinqiao and Huamu near Century Park, Lalaport, Pudong Football Stadium metro area, and office/residential clusters. Availability changes by operator and permit, so treat this as a flexible tech stop.
highlights: Book through Apollo Go (萝卜快跑), PonyPilot+ / Pony.ai, Dazhong, or Gaode/AMap
Board only at mapped pickup/drop-off points inside the service zone
Typical pilots run daytime on weekdays; Jinqiao Apollo has operated roughly 9:00 to 18:00 and Pony around 7:30 to 21:30
places.transport: Once in Pudong, open the app, switch to the Shanghai/Pudong service area if needed, select a nearby pickup pin, then choose a short loop to another allowed stop such as Century Park, Lalaport, or Pudong Football Stadium metro area. Bring passport and use a mainland +86 phone number if registration asks for one.

images.title: File:PVG Lingang DRT Trams 14&19 @ Feidu Road, 2023-09-02.jpg

places.access_note: Bad weather, traffic controls, app registration, or weekend/permit limits can suspend service. If no robotaxi appears, use this as a Pudong tech-area walk and continue to Lujiazui by Metro Line 2.

manual_images.title: File:PVG Lingang DRT Trams 14&19 @ Feidu Road, 2023-09-02.jpg

places.name: Mingzhu Roundabout
places.summary: The circular pedestrian overpass and traffic ring at the heart of Lujiazui, surrounded by the Oriental Pearl, IFC, Super Brand Mall, and the big Pudong towers.
places.transport: Metro Line 2 to Lujiazui Station. Follow signs up to the pedestrian roundabout outside the station.

places.name: Oriental Pearl TV Tower Deck
places.summary: Retro-futurist Shanghai landmark with observation decks over the Huangpu River, Bund, and Lujiazui towers.
highlights: Bund skyline from the Pudong side
places.transport: Walk from Mingzhu Roundabout to the tower entrance. Buy a timed ticket online or at the ticket hall if queues are manageable.
places.access_note: Use this as the Pudong observation deck for the skyline view day.

transfers.instructions: After the robotaxi ride, use its endpoint if it lands near Lujiazui; otherwise ride Metro Line 2 from Century Park or nearby Pudong stations to Lujiazui.
transfers.note: Do not rely on the robotaxi for a cross-city transfer; the service area is bounded.

transfers.instructions: From Mingzhu Roundabout, walk directly to the Oriental Pearl entrance.
transfers.note: Stay in Lujiazui for blue-hour skyline photos before returning to Huangpu.

optional_places.name: Zhujiajiao Water Town
optional_places.summary: Canal water town on Shanghai's western edge, often used as the easy alternative to a Suzhou day. Add only if you want old bridges and lanes more than the Pudong tech morning.
optional_places.transport: Metro Line 17 to Zhujiajiao Station, then taxi or local bus to the old town. From central Shanghai, budget 75 to 100 minutes each way.
optional_places.access_note: This is a swap, not an add-on, unless you are happy to drop the robotaxi experiment or shorten Lujiazui.

days.label: Day 5
days.display_title: Shanghai to Zhangjiajie, train to Fenghuang
days.focus: Fly into Zhangjiajie, transfer to Zhangjiajie West Railway Station, then take the high-speed train to Fenghuang Ancient Town for the evening.
days.transport_note: Use the train instead of the long road transfer: DYG airport to Zhangjiajie West by taxi, high-speed rail to Fenghuanggucheng, then shuttle/taxi into the old town.

start_transfer.title: Hotel to Pudong Airport
start_transfer.instructions: Leave the hotel by 04:30 to be at PVG by 05:30. A pre-booked taxi is simplest for the 06:55 flight; confirm Terminal 2.
start_transfer.note: Keep passports handy for airport and railway ID checks later in the day.

places.name: Fenghuang Ancient Town
places.summary: Fenghuang is best after the train arrival: lantern-lit bridges, old lanes, riverside viewpoints, and Miao/Tujia food around the ancient town core.
highlights: Hong Bridge (Hongqiao Wind and Rain Bridge)
East Gate Tower and old lanes
Wanming Pagoda and Shawan riverside views
Stepping-stone crossing if water levels allow
places.transport: Arrive at Fenghuanggucheng Station, then use the shuttle or taxi into the old town. The homestay is a short walk from the drop-off edge.

foods.name: Miao Sour Fish Soup
foods.description: Whole-fish hotpot in a sour fermented broth with tomatoes, herbs, and chilies. The signature Miao minority dish around Fenghuang.
foods.where: Family-run restaurants on Laoying Shao Street and Hongqiao area in the old town.

foods.name: Fenghuang Ginger Candy
foods.description: Hand-pulled chewy ginger candy folded with peanuts. Watch the makers slap and pull it on wooden pegs along the riverside.
foods.where: Open-front candy shops along the Tuojiang River near Hong Bridge.

stay.label: Tianqingshe Riverview Homestay
stay.address: No. 70 Jinping Road, Tuojiang Town, Fenghuang County, Hunan, China
stay.note: Riverside guesthouse inside the ancient town. Perfect for the lantern-lit evening walk along the Tuojiang River.
stay.transit: From Zhangjiajie Hehua (DYG), taxi to Zhangjiajie West Railway Station, take the high-speed train to Fenghuanggucheng, then shuttle or taxi into the old town.

transfers.instructions: After landing at DYG, taxi about 20 to 30 minutes to Zhangjiajie West Railway Station. Take a high-speed train to Fenghuanggucheng Station; fastest services are roughly 1 hour.
transfers.note: Book Zhangjiajie West to Fenghuanggucheng on Trip.com or 12306 and leave a buffer after the flight.

transfers.instructions: From Fenghuanggucheng Station, take the tourist shuttle or taxi 15 to 25 minutes to Fenghuang Ancient Town, then walk to the riverside homestay.
transfers.note: Cars may stop outside the old-town pedestrian lanes.

after_flight_transfer.title: DYG Airport to Fenghuang by Train
after_flight_transfer.instructions: After landing at Zhangjiajie Hehua Airport, taxi 20 to 30 minutes to Zhangjiajie West Railway Station. Take a high-speed train to Fenghuanggucheng Station, then use the tourist shuttle or taxi 15 to 25 minutes into Fenghuang Ancient Town.
after_flight_transfer.note: Book Zhangjiajie West to Fenghuanggucheng on Trip.com or 12306 and leave a flight-delay buffer before the train.

days.label: Day 6
days.display_title: Fenghuang to Furong day trip, return to Zhangjiajie
days.focus: Leave Fenghuang Ancient Town by train, spend the day in Furong Ancient Town and its waterfall, then train back to Zhangjiajie at night.
days.transport_note: Book Fenghuanggucheng to Furongzhen and Furongzhen to Zhangjiajie West rail tickets in advance. Keep luggage light or store it near the station/old-town entrance.

start_transfer.title: Homestay to Fenghuanggucheng Station
start_transfer.instructions: Leave the old town after breakfast and ride 15 to 25 minutes to Fenghuanggucheng Station. Take the high-speed train toward Furongzhen.
start_transfer.note: Train times vary; aim for a late-morning arrival in Furong so the waterfall town is not rushed.

places.name: Furong Ancient Town
places.summary: A compact Tujia old town built above a wide waterfall. Stone lanes, wooden houses, and river viewpoints make it a strong day trip between Fenghuang and Zhangjiajie.
highlights: Old stone street through the Tujia town
places.transport: Arrive at Furongzhen Railway Station, then take a taxi or local shuttle 10 to 15 minutes to the scenic-area entrance.
places.access_note: The old town is ticketed. Keep your ticket for re-entry around the waterfall viewpoints.

places.name: Furong Waterfall and Riverside Walk
places.summary: The signature curtain waterfall sits directly below the town. Paths usually loop behind or beside the falls depending on water and maintenance conditions.
highlights: Night-light view if staying late enough
places.transport: Walk down through the scenic area from the old street. Return uphill to the entrance for the taxi back to Furongzhen Station.

transfers.instructions: Follow the old street downhill from Furong Ancient Town toward the waterfall viewpoints.
transfers.note: Wear shoes that handle wet stone steps.

stay.label: Zhinanzhen Boutique Inn
stay.address: Guanliping Resettlement Community, Yongding District, Zhangjiajie, Hunan, China
stay.note: Close to the park access roads. Use this base for the National Forest Park day and Tianmen Mountain.
stay.transit: About 30 to 45 minutes by taxi from Zhangjiajie Hehua Airport (DYG). Park shuttles connect to Wulingyuan and Tianmen.

end_transfer.title: Furong to Zhangjiajie
end_transfer.instructions: Return to Furongzhen Station by taxi or shuttle, then take the evening train to Zhangjiajie West. Taxi from Zhangjiajie West to the inn after arrival.
end_transfer.note: This is where the Zhangjiajie stay begins.

days.label: Day 7
days.display_title: Avatar Mountain Route B and 72 Strange Buildings at night
days.focus: Use the most efficient one-day summit route: Wulingyuan East Gate, Bailong Elevator up, Yuanjiajie Avatar viewpoints, Tianzi Mountain, Ten-Mile Gallery, then 72 Strange Buildings at night.
days.transport_note: Best one-day route: Route B / first-timer flow from Wulingyuan East Gate. Eco-bus to Bailong Elevator, elevator up to Yuanjiajie, shuttle to Tianzi Mountain, descend by Tianzi cableway or shuttle, finish Ten-Mile Gallery if daylight remains. Skip Golden Whip Stream unless you drop Yangjiajie or Ten-Mile Gallery.

start_transfer.title: Inn to Wulingyuan East Gate (Wujiayu Ticket Office)
start_transfer.instructions: Leave around 07:00 and aim to enter by 08:00. Use Wulingyuan East Gate / Wujiayu Ticket Office because it has the cleanest shuttle access to Bailong Elevator, Tianzi Mountain, and Ten-Mile Gallery.
start_transfer.note: Buy the park ticket plus Bailong Elevator and Tianzi Cableway add-ons in advance if possible. Carry passport for ticket pickup/checks.

places.name: Bailong Elevator
places.summary: Route B starts with the Bailong Elevator: a 326 m glass outdoor lift that jumps from the valley to the Yuanjiajie plateau in under two minutes. It is the fastest way to reach the Avatar viewpoints.
highlights: Window side is best for the short ride
places.transport: Inside Wulingyuan East Gate, take the free eco-bus to Bailong Elevator Lower Station. Ride the elevator up, then follow signs or shuttle to Yuanjiajie viewpoints.
places.access_note: If the elevator queue is extreme, reverse the route: take Tianzi Mountain Cableway up first, then use Bailong Elevator down later.

places.name: Yuanjiajie and Avatar Hallelujah Mountain
places.summary: The core Avatar section. Walk the signed Yuanjiajie loop for Qiankun Pillar / Avatar Hallelujah Mountain, Mihun Platform, and First Bridge Under Heaven.
highlights: Best pillar views of the day
places.transport: From Bailong Elevator upper station, take the short summit shuttle or walk to Yuanjiajie. Follow the one-way viewpoint loop, then exit at Yuanjiajie Tianqiao Station for the shuttle toward Tianzi Mountain.

images.title: File:Stunning rock formations in Bailong Yuanjiajie scenic area.jpg

manual_images.title: File:Stunning rock formations in Bailong Yuanjiajie scenic area.jpg

places.access_note: This is the non-negotiable part of the day. If fog is heavy, wait 10 to 20 minutes at a viewpoint; visibility can open suddenly.

places.name: Tianzi Mountain
places.summary: The big panorama section after Yuanjiajie. Use the shuttle to Tianzi Mountain for West Sea Stone Forest, Yubi Feng / Imperial Brush Peaks, and Fairy Scattering Flowers.
places.transport: At Yuanjiajie Tianqiao Station, take the eco-bus toward Tianzi Mountain / He Long Park. After sightseeing, descend by Tianzi Mountain Cableway if operating, then shuttle to Ten-Mile Gallery.
places.access_note: Do not miss the last descent transport. Ask staff for the final Tianzi cableway/shuttle time when you arrive.

places.name: Ten-Mile Gallery
places.summary: Lower-valley finish after the summit loop. The Shili Hualang / Ten-Mile Gallery mini-train passes named formations like Three Sisters Peaks; walking is possible if queues are light.
places.transport: After Tianzi descent, take the eco-bus to Ten-Mile Gallery. Ride the mini-train one way or walk the flat valley path, then shuttle back to Wulingyuan East Gate.

images.title: File:武陵源景区十里画廊风光 - panoramio (3).jpg

manual_images.title: File:张家界十里画廊小火车.jpeg

places.access_note: If daylight is low, choose Ten-Mile Gallery over Golden Whip Stream because it is shorter and easier to exit from Wulingyuan.

places.name: Golden Whip Stream
places.best_time: Optional late afternoon only if you cut another section
places.summary: Beautiful valley trail, but a full Golden Whip Stream walk is too much on a one-day Avatar route. Use only a short Water Winding Four Gates / lower-valley segment if you have time.
highlights: Best as a separate half-day if possible
places.transport: Use eco-bus links between Water Winding Four Gates and the Forest Park/South Gate area. For this itinerary, treat it as a short optional segment rather than a full 7.5 km walk.

foods.name: Tujia Sanxiaguo (Three-Layer Pot)
foods.description: Tujia clay-pot stew layered with smoked pork, root vegetables, and chilies. Heavy, smoky, and warming after a day on the trails.
foods.where: Local Tujia restaurants in Zhangjiajie city or Wulingyuan town.

places.access_note: For one day, Golden Whip Stream is the first thing to cut if queues, fog, or tired legs slow the summit loop.

places.name: Zhangjiajie 72 Strange Buildings
places.summary: Modern Tujia-style night landmark known as 72 Qilou / 72 Strange Buildings. The main tower lights up after dark with food streets, performances, and a dramatic stilt-house facade.
highlights: Good full-building photo from across Wulingshan Avenue
Easy taxi stop after a rest
places.transport: Return to the inn from Wulingyuan first if possible, then taxi to No. 1 Wulingshan Avenue in Yongding District. Photograph the full tower from across the road rather than only inside the plaza.
places.access_note: This is a modern cultural/night-market complex, not an ancient site. It is best as a fun night-photo stop after the park.

transfers.instructions: Enter Wulingyuan East Gate, take the eco-bus to Bailong Elevator Lower Station, then ride up to the Yuanjiajie plateau.
transfers.note: This is Route B / first-timer flow and gets the Avatar pillars early.

transfers.instructions: Walk the Yuanjiajie loop: Mihun Platform, Qiankun Pillar / Avatar Hallelujah Mountain, and First Bridge Under Heaven. Exit at Yuanjiajie Tianqiao Station.
transfers.note: Avoid backtracking; follow signs toward the Tianzi Mountain shuttle.

transfers.instructions: Ride the summit eco-bus from Yuanjiajie toward Tianzi Mountain / He Long Park for West Sea Stone Forest and Yubi Feng.
transfers.note: If queues are bad, skip Yangjiajie and keep moving to Tianzi.

transfers.instructions: Descend from Tianzi Mountain by cableway if operating, then take the eco-bus to Ten-Mile Gallery.
transfers.note: Confirm the last cableway time when you arrive at Tianzi Mountain.

transfers.instructions: Do Ten-Mile Gallery as a short finish, then shuttle back to Wulingyuan East Gate. Only add Golden Whip Stream if you still have daylight and energy.
transfers.note: For a single day, keep Golden Whip Stream optional.

transfers.instructions: After a rest at the inn, taxi to Zhangjiajie 72 Strange Buildings for night lights and snacks.
transfers.note: Keep this optional if the park day runs long.

optional_places.name: Yangjiajie Scenic Area
optional_places.summary: Quieter peak-wall area between Yuanjiajie and Tianzi. Add it only if you are moving fast; otherwise it makes the one-day route too tight.
highlights: Wulong Village route for adventurous walkers
optional_places.transport: From Yuanjiajie Tianqiao Station, take the summit shuttle toward Yangjiajie. Rejoin the shuttle network afterward for Tianzi Mountain.
optional_places.access_note: Best optional inside the park, but skip if the Bailong Elevator or Yuanjiajie queues eat the morning.

optional_places.name: Huangshi Village (Huangshizhai)
optional_places.summary: High viewpoint area often quieter in the morning, reached by cable car from the Forest Park/South Gate side. It is excellent, but it does not fit cleanly into the Route B day.
highlights: Good bad-weather backup if summit shuttles are crowded
optional_places.transport: Use the Forest Park/South Gate and Huangshi Village cable car. Treat it as a separate half-day, not an add-on after Yuanjiajie/Tianzi.

optional_places.name: Zhangjiajie Grand Canyon Glass Bridge
optional_places.summary: World-famous glass bridge and canyon walk outside the national forest park. Amazing, but logistically separate from the Avatar mountain day.
highlights: Good extra day if you extend Zhangjiajie
optional_places.transport: Book as a separate day trip from Wulingyuan/Zhangjiajie city by private car or tour. Do not combine with Yuanjiajie/Tianzi in one day.

days.label: Day 8
days.display_title: Tianmen Mountain Route A, then night flight to Beijing
days.focus: Book Route A / Line A if available: ride up on the long Tianmen Mountain Cableway, use the summit cliff walks and escalators, visit Heaven's Gate, then come down on the shorter Express Cableway.
days.transport_note: Ask for Route A / Line A (A线): Main Tianmen Mountain Cableway up, Tianmen Cave Express Cableway down. If the upper main cableway section is still under maintenance, Route A becomes main cableway to mid-station + eco-bus on 99 Bends + escalator to the summit, then Express Cableway down.

start_transfer.title: Inn to Tianmen Mountain Cableway Lower Station
start_transfer.instructions: Taxi or Didi to the downtown Tianmen Mountain Cableway Lower Station beside Zhangjiajie Railway Station. Arrive 30 to 45 minutes before opening if possible and book Route A / Line A.
start_transfer.note: Route A is the one that matches your preference: long main cableway up, shorter Express Cableway down. Confirm live operations because cableway maintenance can change the exact sequence.

places.name: Tianmen Mountain Route A / Line A Ascent
places.summary: Route A is the desired direction: start in downtown Zhangjiajie on the main Tianmen Mountain Cableway, the famous 7,455 m cableway. Under current maintenance patterns, the long line may run only to mid-station, then an eco-bus continues up the 99 Bends Road before the escalator system takes you toward the summit.
highlights: 99 Bends Road if modified operations apply
Best match for long-up / short-down preference
places.transport: Buy or collect a Route A ticket at the downtown lower station. Board the main cableway first; if staff route you to mid-station, follow signs to the eco-bus for Tongtian Avenue / 99 Bends, then continue by the mountain escalator system.
places.access_note: This is a cableway, not an elevator. If the full 7.5 km cableway is restored by your date, Route A gives the complete downtown-to-summit ride; if not, it still starts with the longest cableway section available.

places.name: West Line Cliff Walks and Glass Skywalk
places.summary: After the Route A ascent, do the West Line first: cliff-hanging walkways, glass sections, and big views toward the 99 Bends. It is the classic summit loop and easier to fit before the cave descent.
highlights: Coiling Dragon Cliff / glass section if open
places.transport: From the summit/escalator exit, follow signs for West Line / Xixian. Continue toward Tianmen Mountain Temple or loop back depending on time.
places.access_note: Glass sections may close in bad weather. If queues are long, do one glass walkway only and keep moving toward Heaven's Gate.

places.name: Heaven's Gate and Express Cableway Descent
places.summary: Finish at Tianmen Cave / Heaven's Gate. Descend from the summit by the 7-section mountain escalator to the cave area, walk down the 999 steps if your knees allow, then take the shorter Tianmen Cave Express Cableway down to the mountain gate.
places.transport: From the summit, take the included upper escalators down to Tianmen Cave. Walk down the 999 steps to the plaza, or pay for the lower escalator if needed. Then walk about 500 m to the Express Cableway station for the short descent and shuttle back toward downtown.
places.access_note: For your requested direction, choose Route A. Route B is the reverse direction: Express Cableway up and main cableway/bus down.

transfers.instructions: Start at the downtown lower station and take the main Tianmen Mountain Cableway up. If maintenance is active, ride to mid-station, then transfer to the eco-bus up 99 Bends and escalators toward the summit.
transfers.note: This is the longest-up option.

transfers.instructions: Do the West Line cliff path and one glass skywalk, then loop toward the summit/escalator connection.
transfers.note: West Line is the most efficient summit route if you only have one day before a flight.

transfers.instructions: Take the mountain escalators down to Tianmen Cave, descend the 999 Steps or lower escalator, then take the shorter Express Cableway down to the mountain gate.
transfers.note: This is the shorter-down option; shuttle or taxi back to the city afterward.

end_transfer.title: Tianmen Mountain to DYG Airport
end_transfer.instructions: Return to the inn by around 18:00, collect bags, eat, and leave for Zhangjiajie Hehua Airport around 19:30 for the 22:30 Beijing flight.
end_transfer.note: Keep the flight card after the Tianmen route so it reads as the transition to Beijing.

optional_places.name: Yunmeng Fairy Summit (Yunmeng Xianding)
optional_places.summary: Highest viewpoint area on Tianmen Mountain, reached by the small Forest Sightseeing Cable Car from near Tianmen Mountain Temple. Add only if visibility is good.
highlights: City and ridge panorama on clear days
optional_places.transport: From the West/East Line junction near Tianmen Mountain Temple, take the Forest Sightseeing Cable Car to Yunmeng Xianding, then return to the main loop.
optional_places.access_note: Skip in fog or if you need to protect the airport transfer buffer.

optional_places.name: East Line Cliff Walk
optional_places.summary: Quieter summit loop with more cliff paths. Good if you arrive early and queues are light, but the West Line plus Heaven's Gate is the priority.
optional_places.transport: After the West Line, continue onto the East Line instead of returning directly to the escalator, then loop back toward Tianmen Cave descent.
image_queries: Tianmen Mountain east line cliff walk
optional_places.access_note: Do not add this if you are behind schedule; the night flight makes time control important.

days.label: Day 9
days.display_title: Badaling Great Wall, Longqingxia Gorge and Houhai
days.focus: First Beijing day: go north to Badaling Great Wall, continue to Longqingxia Gorge, then return to the city for Houhai Lake at night.
days.transport_note: Book a private car for the wall + gorge loop. It is a long day after the late arrival, but it avoids backtracking through public transport.

start_transfer.title: Hotel to Badaling Great Wall
start_transfer.instructions: Start around 07:30 from the hotel and drive north to Badaling. A private car lets you continue to Longqingxia Gorge afterward instead of returning to Beijing first.
start_transfer.note: Badaling is the easiest logistics choice but crowded. Book tickets in advance and bring passports.

places.name: Badaling Great Wall
places.summary: The most accessible restored Great Wall section, with broad ramparts, strong views, and easy cable-car options. Go early to beat the biggest crowds.
highlights: Cable car or pulley options depending on route
places.transport: Private car is easiest from the hotel. Public transport is possible by high-speed train to Badaling Great Wall Station, but less convenient if continuing to Longqingxia.
places.access_note: Carry passports for ticket checks. Start early; Badaling gets crowded fast.

places.name: Longqingxia Gorge
places.summary: Longqingxia / Dragon Gorge is a reservoir canyon with boat cruises through steep northern-China cliffs. It pairs well with Badaling because both sit in Yanqing.
highlights: Boat cruise through the gorge if operating
Dragon escalator up the dam area
Cable car viewpoint if time allows
Cooler mountain air than central Beijing
places.transport: Drive from Badaling to Longqingxia in about 40 to 60 minutes. Ask the driver to wait. Inside, prioritize entrance + boat ticket first; add cable car/viewpoint only if the boat line is short.
places.access_note: Arrive as early as possible after Badaling. Boat schedules and water activities vary by season/weather; if closed, use the gorge viewpoints and head back to Beijing earlier.

places.name: Houhai Lake
places.summary: Houhai is the relaxed Shichahai lake-and-hutong evening zone. Use it for a soft landing after the wall/gorge day: lakeside walk, Yinding Bridge, dinner, and bars if you want them.
highlights: Easy dinner after the long drive
Optional bar street without committing to nightlife
places.transport: Have the driver drop you at Shichahai/Houhai, or rest at the hotel first and taxi over. Metro Line 8 to Shichahai also works if you are returning independently.

transfers.instructions: Continue from Badaling to Longqingxia Gorge by car.
transfers.note: This is the reason to avoid doing Badaling by train today.

transfers.instructions: Return from Longqingxia to central Beijing and get dropped near Houhai or back at the hotel.
transfers.note: Expect 90 minutes or more back into the city depending on traffic.

stay.label: PiaoHome Chain Hotel (Guomao Donghua)
stay.address: No. 1-6, Building 4, Chaijiawan, Chaoyang District, Beijing, 100025, China
stay.note: Chaoyang base for the Beijing classics. Late check-in expected because the Zhangjiajie flight lands after midnight.
stay.transit: Closest stations are Sihui and Sihui East (Lines 1 and Batong). Quick taxi to CBD and Wangfujing. Daxing Airport is about 60 to 75 minutes by taxi.

days.label: Day 10
days.display_title: Zhengyangmen, Forbidden City, Jingshan, Summer Palace and CBD
days.focus: Second Beijing day: old central-axis landmarks, palace roofs from Jingshan, Summer Palace, then the CBD around CCTV Headquarters.
days.transport_note: Book Forbidden City tickets in advance. This is a packed day, so start early and use taxis between distant zones when needed.

start_transfer.title: Hotel to Zhengyangmen / Qianmen
start_transfer.instructions: Go early to Qianmen/Zhengyangmen. From there continue north toward the Forbidden City entrance route without scheduling the square as a stop.
start_transfer.note: Carry passports for palace security and ticket checks.

places.name: Zhengyangmen (Qianmen Gate)
places.summary: Historic gate tower on Beijing's central axis, marking the old front gate of the Inner City and the Qianmen area.
highlights: Easy link toward the palace route
places.transport: Metro Line 2 to Qianmen Station or taxi to Zhengyangmen. Keep the stop short so the Forbidden City day stays on schedule.

places.name: Forbidden City (Palace Museum)
places.summary: Follow the south-to-north palace axis: Meridian Gate, Hall of Supreme Harmony, central courtyards, Palace of Heavenly Purity, Imperial Garden, then exit at Gate of Divine Prowess for Jingshan.
highlights: Three Great Halls on the central axis
places.transport: Pre-book tickets online and enter at Meridian Gate. The palace route is one-way south to north, so do not plan to backtrack to Qianmen afterward.
places.access_note: Mandatory online booking the day before. Closed on Mondays.
image_queries: Palace Museum Beijing Hall of Supreme Harmony

foods.name: Peking Duck
foods.description: Lacquered roast duck carved tableside. Eat the crisp skin with sugar, then wrap the meat in thin pancakes with scallion and hoisin.
foods.where: Siji Minfu (Wangfujing branch) for a more refined plate, or Da Dong (Jinbao Place) for the modern version.

images.title: File:Peking duck by Mr Wabu in Beijing.jpg
File:The Forbidden City - View from Coal Hill.jpg

places.name: Jingshan Park
places.summary: Artificial hill made from the soil dug for the palace moat. The summit pavilion gives the classic axial sunset view of the Forbidden City roofs.
places.transport: Walk across from the north gate of the Forbidden City to Jingshan's south entrance.
image_queries: Jingshan Park Beijing Forbidden City view

foods.name: Zha Jiang Mian
foods.description: Hand-cut wheat noodles topped with a salty fermented soybean and pork sauce, plus julienned vegetables you toss in.
foods.where: Old Beijing Zhajiangmian Daiwang in Qianmen, or any Lao Beijing noodle house.

images.title: File:The Forbidden City - View from Coal Hill.jpg

places.name: Summer Palace
places.summary: For a packed day, use a focused Summer Palace route: North Palace Gate or East Palace Gate, Long Corridor, Kunming Lake edge, Marble Boat, then exit near the easiest taxi/metro point.
highlights: Longevity Hill view if energy remains
places.transport: Taxi is fastest from Jingshan. If using metro, take Line 4 to Beigongmen for North Palace Gate. Use a 2-hour loop and exit before evening traffic gets heavy.

images.title: File:Longevity Hill of the Summer Palace.jpg

places.access_note: Large site. Do not try to cover every corner; choose a 2-hour loop around the lake and hill.

manual_images.title: File:Longevity Hill of the Summer Palace.jpg

places.name: Beijing CBD and CCTV Headquarters
places.summary: End in Guomao/CBD for the modern contrast: CCTV Headquarters exterior, China World towers, and bright business-district streets near your hotel side of town.
places.transport: Taxi from Summer Palace is easiest. By metro, use Line 4/10 toward Guomao. The CCTV building is viewed from public sidewalks; there is no tourist interior visit.

transfers.instructions: Go from Zhengyangmen/Qianmen toward the Forbidden City visitor entry route.
transfers.note: Keep this as a transit path only, not a planned stop.

transfers.instructions: Exit the Forbidden City at the north gate and cross to Jingshan Park for the rooftop view.
transfers.note: Keep enough time for the Summer Palace transfer.

transfers.instructions: Transfer from Jingshan to the Summer Palace. Taxi is fastest for this packed route.
transfers.note: Use Beigongmen or East Palace Gate depending on ticket availability.

transfers.instructions: Return from the Summer Palace to Guomao/CBD for the CCTV Headquarters exterior and dinner.
transfers.note: This places the modern business district at the end near the Beijing hotel base.

optional_places.name: Temple of Heaven
optional_places.summary: Imperial ritual complex with the Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests. It is one of Beijing's most beautiful sites, but only fits if you drop or shorten another stop.
highlights: Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests
Morning locals exercising in the park
optional_places.transport: Metro Line 5 to Tiantandongmen. Best before Zhengyangmen, but it makes the day very full.
image_queries: Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests

images.title: File:Temple of Heaven, Beijing, China - 010 edit.jpg

optional_places.access_note: Add only by shortening Summer Palace or CBD; do not squeeze it into the middle of the Forbidden City route.

optional_places.name: Beiwu Park
optional_places.summary: Quiet free park west of the Summer Palace with rice fields, water, boardwalks, and a softer local feel. Good decompression if Summer Palace crowds are heavy.
highlights: Quiet local park near Summer Palace
optional_places.transport: Take the Xijiao Line to Summer Palace West Gate Station and walk about 500 m, or taxi from Summer Palace West Gate.
optional_places.access_note: Use as a crowd-escape swap near Summer Palace, not as an extra if the palace day is already running late.

days.label: Day 11
days.focus: Early flight south. China leg ends and the Taiwan itinerary picks up the same morning.
days.transport_note: Pre-book an airport transfer the night before. Beijing Capital (PEK) is the right airport for HU7987.

start_transfer.title: Hotel to Beijing Capital Airport
start_transfer.instructions: Pickup by 04:30 from the hotel. Direct taxi to PEK Terminal 2 (Hainan Airlines) takes about 30 to 45 minutes at that hour.
start_transfer.note: Confirm Terminal 2 for HU7987. Allow time for the international check-in and Taiwan entry forms.
